gary_2026_07_10_ai_briefing_by_startup_g.title: AI Briefing By Startup Grind - C-Suite Gap In AI Readiness
gary_2026_07_10_ai_briefing_by_startup_g.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_10_ai_briefing_by_startup_g.summary: Gary's Guide: The AI Briefing is a high-velocity roundtable for decision-makers brought to you by Startup Grind. We bypass the hype by dissecting one high-signal industry deck with a room of vetted founders, operators, & enterprise executives. No lectures-just a 60-minute deep-dive into the tactics & data defining your next quarter. The gap between organizations that have built accountability structures around their AI & those that haven't is now measurable. Revenue growth reaches 58% among fully integrated organizations. Among those still piloting, top-line growth is only 15%. This session uses Grant Thornton's data to examine what it actually means to own an AI investment: who makes decisions, who measures outcomes, & what it costs when leadership can't agree on whether the organization is ready. We'll focus on the C-suite misalignment finding: CIOs & COOs within the same organization hold fundamentally different views on AI readiness, & the data shows that gap is producing real organizational failure.

gary_2026_07_10_build_ai_customer_agents.title: Build AI Customer Agents That Remember & Resolve Issues
gary_2026_07_10_build_ai_customer_agents.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_10_build_ai_customer_agents.summary: Gary's Guide: Bring one real customer conversation use case. Leave with a practical AI pilot plan for chat, voice, WhatsApp, CRM, & human handoff. Join us for a hands-on workshop hosted by Dean from Chanl.AI & Benjamin from Vonage. Most companies don't need another AI demo. They need to know which customer conversations are worth automating, which ones should stay human, & how to launch an AI agent pilot without creating a bad customer experience. In this hands-on workshop, you'll bring one real support, sales, onboarding, scheduling, billing, or service workflow from your business & redesign it for the AI era. Together, we'll map the conversation, identify where AI can help, define the handoff to human teams, choose the right channel, & create a simple pilot plan you can actually use. This is not a coding class - no coding experience needed. It is a practical working session for teams exploring how AI agents can improve customer conversations. What you'll work on Pick the right first customer conversation for AI Create agents for Text or Voice - look at nuances between different channels - chat, voice, WhatsApp, web, or human handoff Map the customer journey from first message to resolution Define what the AI should answer, ask, remember, or escalate Identify the data, knowledge base, CRM, or support tools needed Create test cases for bad answers, escalation failures, & edge cases Define success metrics like resolution rate, response time, CSAT, QA, & cost per contact Leave with a 30-day roadmap for piloting AI agents for your company or startup Who it's for This workshop is designed for: CIOs & technology leaders exploring AI pilots Product managers designing AI-powered workflows CX & support leaders improving customer operations Call center & contact center teams Startup founders building or evaluating voice/chat agents Operators who want practical AI use cases, not hype No coding experience is required. What you'll leave with By the end, you'll have a clear plan for deploying customer conversations agents pilot for your company. Agenda 4:30pm - 5pm arrive & network, pizza & snacks 5:00pm - 7:00pm workshop 7:00pm - 7:30 Network, ask questions, make friends Topics for Workshop 1. What makes a good AI customer conversation A quick teardown of bad bots, useful agents, & strong human handoff. How to monitor agents in real-time, or get alerts. 2. Pick your use case, common use cases for AI Score one real customer conversation based on volume, pain, risk, data readiness, & business value. 3. Map the flow Design what the customer says, what the AI should do, when it should ask questions, & when it should escalate. 4. Choose the channel & agent goals Decide whether the workflow belongs on chat, voice, WhatsApp, web, SMS, or on Slack with your human support team. 5. Design the handoff (Memory) Define when the AI stops, what the human receives, & how the customer avoids repeating themselves. 6. Test before launch Create test cases for hallucinations, policy mistakes, angry customers, missing data, voice interruptions, & failed escalations. 7. Build your 30-day pilot plan Turn the workflow into a simple implementation brief your team can use after the workshop. Hosted by Chanl AI & Fabrik Chanl AI is building infrastructure for AI-powered customer conversations across chat, voice, & human teams.

gary_2026_07_12_creationship_sundays_ai_.title: Creationship Sundays - AI For Creative Good
gary_2026_07_12_creationship_sundays_ai_.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_12_creationship_sundays_ai_.summary: Gary's Guide: A weekly imagine-a-thon for creative good. Come build the next generation of ideas this world needs. Sunday evenings, the back room of a vegan restaurant on East 6th Street fills with people drawn to the intersection of positive social change & new technology. Four hours later, we've met each other, ideated together, & built something real. Some teams form for the night. Some keep meeting, sunday after sunday, building what belongs in the future we want to live in. We don't curate the guest list. Past weeks brought together a founder, a prompt engineer, a doula, a muralist, & someone restoring old radios. The room simply takes the shape of whoever shows up. One tool we use is Vibecoding: our new capacity, with AI, to build applications & digital projects with natural language in the form of text prompts. That said, there are many ways to create & create with others. How the evening unfolds: A community member shares something they can't stop thinking about, teaches a concept or a possible solution to a social conundrum, leads us through an activity that might be valuable to us individually & collectively. Then, we pitch our half-formed projects. Teams form naturally around whatever sparks each person's interest. For the rest of the time, we build, we nosh, & we share what we made. What we're actually doing: We are practicing prototyping & building new worlds. The world outside is largely playing a finite game, optimizing for extraction, scale, & speed. In this room, we step out of that. We practice creative attention, trust, & infinite thinking. We focus fiercely on one thing, alongside people who think nothing like us. The work is sitting shoulder to shoulder with erstwhile strangers (who become community members), sharing our imagination, & building something better together. The relationships that come from this-we call them creationships. Each week looks a little bit different. This week #14: Please try to arrive on time! How a Sunday goes 4:30 - doors open, settle in, say hello, order something 5:00 - opening share / activity 5:30 - intros & form projects groups for the day 6:00 - explore, make, create something 8:00 - show-and-tell You leave inspired, a little changed, & with people you'll see again. Some things we're making here Kula - a gifting platform Vibe Cadaver - an exquisite-corpse game Kitty Social - a shared fund Ocean Agentics - a high seas treaty site NotAmazon - local alternatives Whoever shows up is who the afternoon is for. The room shapes itself around the people in it. Come as you are. Hosted by Dear Crisis dearcrisis.com

gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.title: Understanding Work, Agency & Stewardship In Age Of Generative & Agentic AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.summary: Gary's Guide: All Tech Is Human (ATIH), in partnership with the Notre Dame-IBM Tech Ethics Lab, will host a two-part workshop series focused on perceptions, adoption, & experience with Agentic AI systems across the workforce. Led by Jen Weedon, ATIH Braintrust member, this series aims to surface both strategic & lived insights from across organizational levels, generating a more holistic set of data points to inform responsible research & practice in the enterprise space. On Tuesday, July 14, the conversation moves toward organizational leadership & governance. We will bring together 20-30 senior executives who are currently standing at the crossroads of automation. Whether you are navigating the complexities of adoption, facing regulatory bottlenecks, dealing with internal reorgs or downsizing, and/or weighing the decision to automate core processes, this executive roundtable offers a high-level forum to discuss the ethics of efficiency & the push towards AI-first or AI-native transformations. This workshop aims to surface the process, knowledge, tensions, & skill gaps. Together, we will explore how leadership can drive innovation while maintaining the trust & integrity essential to the modern enterprise. Express your interest below. This workshop will be four hours long. The workshop series will explore how different types of organizations are approaching Agentic AI - from those whose systems are already deployed, to those preparing for rollout, to those still in exploratory phases. By comparing lived experiences with Agentic AI to expectations across roles & organizational contexts, the workshops will surface concerns, decision-making realities, & unintended consequences. The goal is to understand better the dynamics shaping acute & sustained adoption, governance, & the cascading effects on the workforce. Day 2: Agentic AI from the Leadership Perspective (Organizational & Ecosystem Leaders) Agenda forthcoming

gary_2026_07_15_ai_adoption_open_mic_hap.title: AI Adoption Open Mic & Happy Hour
gary_2026_07_15_ai_adoption_open_mic_hap.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_15_ai_adoption_open_mic_hap.place: Event Space at SPACS, 149 W 27th St,, 3rd Fl
gary_2026_07_15_ai_adoption_open_mic_hap.summary: Gary's Guide: New York doesn't need another boring AI panel. So, we're hosting an open mic night instead. To celebrate PAIR's New York office launch, we're throwing a party to bring together our US & London teams with enterprise AI operators in the city to compare notes on how AI is actually making a difference (and where it isn't). Our first AI Adoption Open Mic Night was an unexpected hit. What started as a funny idea turned into something people actually loved, so we knew we had to do it again. This time, we're making it bigger, bringing the community together for drinks, dinner, & a fast-paced open mic where each speaker gets 100 seconds to share one real AI use case & its impact. The strongest use case of the night will win a $500 cash prize & a 3-year PAIR licence worth over $800. And even if you don't win the grand prize, expect to walk away with new connections in the AI community, & ideas you can take back to your team. The first open mic night sold out fast, so RSVP now to secure your spot. P.S. No pressure to participate in the open mic, you're very welcome to just come along, meet others working on AI adoption, & hear what people are actually doing with AI in practice. Agenda 6:00 PM - Drinks & food on arrival 6:30 PM - Intro from our MCs (and co-founders!) 6:45 PM - AI Adoption Open Mic 7:15 PM - $500 prize for the strongest use case 7:30 PM - Conversations & networking 9:00 PM - Wrapping up the evening About PAIR PAIR is the leading AI adoption platform for enterprise. We help organisations build real AI capability across their workforce, give leaders visibility into adoption, scale the workflows that create value, & prove the return on AI investment. PAIR was founded by James Kuht & Mark Mitchell, whose backgrounds span AI, government, specialist military units & large-scale workforce adoption.

gary_2026_07_16_product_summit.title: Product Summit
gary_2026_07_16_product_summit.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_07_16_product_summit.summary: Gary's Guide: A one-day summit for product leaders & professionals at the edge of innovation. Themes being explored: Leading Product Strategy Through Disruption Frameworks for navigating market uncertainty, maintaining cross-functional alignment, & building resilient product roadmaps in rapidly evolving industries. Building Trust in AI-Driven Products Best practices for integrating intelligent systems & autonomous features while prioritizing user confidence, data privacy, & transparent user experiences. Unlocking Product-Led Growth & Retention How to build self-sustaining ecosystems by combining continuous user discovery, community engagement, & operational discipline to drive enduring product growth. "Shifting Left" in the Product Lifecycle Practical frameworks for accelerating time-to-value & reducing risk by bringing continuous discovery, technical feasibility, & quality assurance to the earliest stages of development. The Product Manager of the Future How autonomous agents, large language models, & advanced tooling are reshaping product workflows, strategic decision-making, & the PM's role in human-computer interaction. gary_2026_07_25_nj_code_coffee.summary: Gary's Guide: Calling all New Jersey techies to the monthly NJ Code & Coffee tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus! Code & Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious). That includes operators, founders, & investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... all are welcome! People of all skill & experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, & many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you! Come grab a cup of coffee & meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, make friends! Agenda: 2pm: Doors open, networking starts 2:30pm: Intro circle 3pm: networking continues 5pm: event ends The Intro Circle Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle: We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, & logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says: *your name & job title/ role in tech/ tech stack, What you can help others with, *What you would like help with, Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool. After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized. Community Discord - come hang out before & after!: www.codeandcoffee.chat The Venue Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming & a powerful network. Headquartered in the City of Newark, their share-space provides resources for startups, coaching for tech companies, & a wealth of intellectuals to collaborate & build with. The next wave in enterprises is being born within their walls. Become a part of the story: [EqualSpace.co/](https://equalspace.co/)

jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.title: Around the World Watch Party 2026
jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.label: Parks / Outdoor
jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.summary: The “Around the World Watch Party” is a festival centered on a live broadcast of the World Cup Final. The event will transform Claremont Avenue (between West Side Avenue and Mallory Avenue) into an immersive “world village,” featuring curated cultural zones representing different regions. This program is designed to bring together residents, local businesses, and cultural organizations in a free and accessible public space that promotes community engagement and cultural exchange. Event Policies No re-entry prohibited. Supervision required for children under 12. Event may be photographed or recorded. Allowed/Prohibited items for Event Prohibited Items Outside Food or Beverages Weapons/No CCPS Illegal Substances Drones Fireworks or Explosives Pets (except service animals) Lawn Chairs SECURITY SCREENING REQUIRED All guests are subject to magnetometer screening and bag inspection. Note that any items you bring to the Around the World Watch Party could be subject to search. Please remember to keep bags, packages, strollers, etc. in sight. Do not leave any belongings unattended at any time.
